commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a dedicated OpenAI cookie deadline dispatch queue, switches three timeout callbacks to it, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: no. local runtime reproduction was attempted because this is a read-only review, but source inspection shows current main schedules the timeout callbacks on the same global pool that can be blocked by cookie work. The PR body also identifies the macOS Intel CI lane in https://github.com/steipete/CodexBar/pull/1573 as the observed trigger.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed because no line-level defect was found; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer/check handling for an owner-authored PR.

target.title: Fix cookie import timeout scheduling
target.body:
## Summary

- schedule OpenAI dashboard cookie-import deadlines on a dedicated queue
- keep timeout delivery responsive when browser cookie work blocks the shared worker pool
- document the reliability fix

## Proof

- `swift test --filter OpenAIDashboardBrowserCookieImporterTests`
- 20 repeated `OpenAIDashboardBrowserCookieImporterTests` suite runs
- exact local CI shard 0/4: all 121 groups passed
- `make check`
- `autoreview --mode local --parallel-tests ...`: clean, 0.86

## Context

The macOS Intel CI lane for #1573 exposed this shared-main race: blocking cache work completed before a delayed timeout callback that shared the global worker pool. Other #1573 lanes passed; this fix is intentionally separate from that contributor PR.
target.author: steipete
